A CONNECTED RENTAL JOURNEY

From first lead to signed agreement

Coordinate the customer journey while keeping each operational step connected to the same rental.

  1. 01

    Lead

    Keep account, contact, and facility details together as a new lead takes shape.

    →
  2. 02

    Reserved

    Coordinate move dates, the selected unit or package, and stored-item details.

    →
  3. 03

    Verified

    Support manual and linked identity-verification steps within the rental journey.

    →
  4. 04

    Paid

    Complete card payment and keep the invoice record connected to the rental.

    →
  5. 05

    Signed

    Send an electronic agreement and track its signature status alongside the account.

BUILT AROUND DAILY OPERATIONS

The operational pieces, connected

Bring the working parts of a self-storage operation into one system, organized around facilities and the rental journey.

01

Facilities and inventory

Organize facility details, units, sizes, packages, merchandise, and rentable availability by facility.

UnitSizeStatus
A-1085 × 10Available
B-21410 × 10Available
02

Pricing and insurance

Configure pricing groups, fees, taxes, and insurance settings that support a rental.

Base priceFeesTaxInsurance
03

Tenants and rentals

Connect lead, tenant, reservation, rental, contact, and stored-property details.

LeadTenantRental
04

Cards and billing records

Support card payments and keep invoices, refunds, dues, and transaction records connected.

Invoice statusPayment recorded
Paid
05

Verification and documents

Support identity-verification and electronic-signature workflows, with links sent by email or SMS.

Signed
06

Staff and permissions

Give team members access based on their responsibilities and assigned facilities.

OPFMADFacility access

ONE SYSTEM, FACILITY BY FACILITY

Keep every facility aligned without flattening the details.

Organize branding, inventory, pricing settings, and staff access by facility—then connect customer-facing websites to selected facilities and configured inventory.

  • Single- and multi-facility organization
  • Facility-scoped inventory and commercial rules
  • Configurable websites and custom-domain workflows

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

A clearer view of SlfStrg

What operators need to know about the platform and how it fits their work.

Who is SlfStrg for?

SlfStrg is built for self-storage owners, operators, operations leads, facility managers, and administrative teams.

Can it support more than one facility?

Yes. SlfStrg supports single- and multi-facility organization, including facility-scoped inventory, pricing settings, websites, users, and permissions.

Which parts of the rental journey can be managed?

SlfStrg connects leads, tenant and reservation details, move dates, units, stored items, identity-verification steps, card payments, invoices, and electronic agreements.

Can operator websites be configured by facility?

Yes. Customer-facing websites can be configured around selected facilities, including branding, domains, and configured inventory.

How can I evaluate SlfStrg?

Book a demo to see how SlfStrg fits your operation.
